name: Kidston Pumped Storage Hydro Project 250MW
notable: First pumped hydro registered in NEM in ~40 years. Former Kidston Gold Mine site. 8-hour storage.

timeline.title: EPC contractor selected
timeline.detail: McConnell Dowell / John Holland JV selected

timeline.title: Financial close — A$777M
timeline.detail: Financial close reached; construction NTP issued

timeline.title: Registered in NEM
timeline.detail: First pumped hydro registered in NEM in ~40 years

timeline.title: COD estimate updated
timeline.detail: COD changed from 2027-01 to 2027-01-01 (source: AEMO Generation Information)

eis_specs.document_title: Kidston Clean Energy Hub — Pumped Storage Hydro EIS (QLD Coordinator-General / EPBC 2017/8065)
eis_specs.connection_substation_name: Kidston 275 kV substation (new on-site), connected to Powerlink via ~275 km line to Ross 275 kV substation (Townsville)
eis_specs.connection_distance_note: ~275 km of new 275 kV HVAC transmission from Kidston (near Mt Garnet, North QLD) to Powerlink Ross substation (Townsville). One of Australia's longest recent new single-circuit transmission lines.
eis_specs.connection_augmentation: CRITICAL: New 275 km x 275 kV transmission line from remote Kidston to Powerlink Ross (Townsville). This transmission represents the major capex challenge — site is isolated from QLD grid. Powerlink and Genex faced prolonged negotiations over transmission access and cost allocation. QLD government provided support via direct investment.
eis_specs.notes: 250 MW / 900 MWh (3.6-hour) pumped hydro using two former Kidston gold mine open pits (K1 upper reservoir, K2 lower reservoir), near Mt Garnet, Far North QLD. Developer: Genex Power. Hydro OEM: ANDRITZ Hydro (2 x 125 MW reversible Francis pump-turbines). EPC: McConnell Dowell / John Holland JV. QLD EIS approved 2017 + EPBC. Under construction 2024. World's first large-scale pumped hydro reusing former open-cut mine pits as reservoirs. ~220 m head difference between K1 and K2. Genex also operates 50 MW solar + 50 MW wind at Kidston.
